summary: Google adopts MCP for Gemini, Grok 4.5 launches at lower prices, OpenHands releases agent profiles, and coding agents face real-world friction around evals, debugging, and cost management. | Official vendor releases and research: GPT-5.6 family, Mistral Vibe/Studio, Claude Code containment, Kotlin benchmark, ADK Go 2.0, sandbox funding (Daytona, E2B), and agent security/benchmark papers. | GitHub OSS releases show heavy churn in mainstream agent coding tools (Claude Code, Cline, OpenHands, Qwen-Code) and memory/observability infra (Mem0, Langfuse). Two new sandbox projects (microVM forking, Podman rootless) signal growing operator concern with security and scalability. Edge releases from Cloudflare Agents and E2B indicate platform infrastructure maturing. Missing: dedicated user_workflow field reports from social sources (avoided here per shard). | Package ecosystem shows active agent memory, MCP tooling, and eval infrastructure releases. Vercel's agent-eval playground and CodeBuddy SDK are notable mainstream signals. Several memory/infra primitives (stashai, greprag, agenticow) and MCP testing tools are gaining traction.
